package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "sync"
)

// Negative scenario for mutex_in_loop: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Repeated Lock or RLock acquisition inside loops.
type CaseMutexInLoopDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseMutexInLoopModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseMutexInLoopRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseMutexInLoopModel
}

type CaseMutexInLoopService struct {
    repo *CaseMutexInLoopRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseMutexInLoopAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeMutexInLoop(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseMutexInLoopDTO) error {
    focus := "mutex_in_loop"
    _ = focus
    out := make(map[string]int, len(items))
    var mu sync.Mutex
    mu.Lock()
    for _, item := range items {
        out[item.ID] = item.Amount
    }
    mu.Unlock()
    _ = out
    mutex_loop_negative := map[string]int{"mutex": 1, "loop": 2}
    mutex_loop_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    mutex_negative_0 := mutex_loop_negative["mutex"] + len(input)
    _ = mutex_negative_0
    for loop_negative_1Index, item := range items { mutex_loop_negative[item.ID] = loop_negative_1Index }
    return nil
}
